events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess duct installation: flex duct sags between hangers, metal-to-flex joints wrapped only with gray cloth duct tape; no mastic, UL-listed foil tape, or mechanical draw bands visible. Homeowner says air comes out but room cold. Stage rough-complete. Need decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? Duct leakage and restricted airflow causing comfort/energy, not life safety. Could be medium. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? value not relevant. sound_source etc default empty string/0/list. Need findings: excessive flex duct sag, unsupported flex duct; joints sealed with non-UL cloth duct tape, no mechanical draw bands, likely leakage. Actions: support flex duct per IMC 603/SMACNA (max 4-5 ft supports, max 1/2 inch sag per foot or no contact), replace tape with UL 181A-P/B-FX listed tape or mastic, install draw bands, pressure test or airflow check. References: IMC 603.10 (flex duct supports), IMC 603.9 (seal class maybe), SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, ACCA Manual D. Need ensure JSON valid.

  "findings": [
    "Flexible duct is visibly sagging between supports, indicating inadequate hanger spacing/support.",
    "Metal-to-flex connections are sealed only with gray cloth duct tape, which is not UL 181 listed for duct sealing.",
    "No mechanical draw bands/clamps are visible at the flex-to-metal joints.",
    "Visible conditions indicate significant duct leakage and possible airflow restriction, consistent with the cold room complaint."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install additional supports/hangers per IMC 603 and SMACNA to eliminate sag and maintain duct shape.",
    "Remove non-listed cloth duct tape from all joints.",
    "Secure flex duct to metal collars with mechanical draw bands.",
    "Seal metal-to-flex joints with UL 181A-P/B-FX listed foil tape or mastic.",
    "Perform duct leakage test and airflow/room temperature verification after repairs."
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation fails because flexible duct must be adequately supported to prevent sagging and airflow restriction, and duct joints must be sealed with approved materials (UL 181 listed tape or mastic) and mechanically fastened. Gray cloth duct tape is not an approved duct sealant, and the lack of draw bands violates standard installation practice.",
  "references": [
    "IMC 603.10 - Flexible duct supports and installation",
    "IMC 603.9 - Duct sealing materials and methods",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards - Flexible duct installation and support",
    "ACCA Manual D - Residential duct design and installation practices"
  ]
}
